tu.prop: Due to its inertness and isotopic signature, helium (the lightest noble gas) is a useful tracer for investigating the processes of storage and transfer of ﬂuids through the crust, including those prior to hazardous earthquakes.

tuv.seg: ARTICLE https://doi.org/10.1038/s43247-022-00549-9 OPEN Earthquakes control the impulsive nature of crustal helium degassing to the atmosphere , Dario Buttitta1,4, Matteo Picozzi2, Michele Paternoster 1,4 & Tony Alfredo Stabile Antonio Caracausi 1✉ 3 ; , : ) ( 0 9 8 7 6 5 4 3 2 1 Deep ﬂuids play active roles during the preparatory phases of large earthquakes and, through their chemical signature, carry information about deep processes within the seismogenic crust.

ARTICLE https://doi.org/10.1038/s43247-022-00549-9 OPEN Les séismes contrôlent la nature impulsive du dégazage de l'hélium crustal dans l'atmosphère, Dario Buttitta1,4, Matteo Picozzi2, Michele Paternoster 1,4 & Tony Alfredo Stabile Antonio Caracausi 1✉ 3 ; , :) ( 0 9 8 7 6 5 4 3 2 Les fluides profonds jouent un rôle actif pendant les phases préparatoires des grands séismes et, grâce à leur signature chimique, transmettent des informations sur les processus profonds au sein de la croûte sismogénique.

tu.prop: ARTICLE https://doi.org/10.1038/s43247-022-00549-9 OPEN Earthquakes control the impulsive nature of crustal helium degassing to the atmosphere , Dario Buttitta1,4, Matteo Picozzi2, Michele Paternoster 1,4 & Tony Alfredo Stabile Antonio Caracausi 1✉ 3 ; , : ) ( 0 9 8 7 6 5 4 3 2 1 Deep ﬂuids play active roles during the preparatory phases of large earthquakes and, through their chemical signature, carry information about deep processes within the seismogenic crust.

Here we analyse a 12-year earthquake cat- alogue from the Irpinia Fault Zone, Italy, to compute the 4He outputs from the seismogenetic fault zones (from 104 to 106 mol y−1 with an annual tenfold variability) and compare these with estimates of long-term helium ﬂux.

tuv.seg: Due to its inertness and isotopic signature, helium (the lightest noble gas) is a useful tracer for investigating the processes of storage and transfer of ﬂuids through the crust, including those prior to hazardous earthquakes.

En raison de son inertie et de sa signature isotopique, l'hélium (le gaz noble le plus léger) est un traceur utile pour étudier les processus de stockage et de transfert des fluides à travers la croûte, y compris ceux antérieurs aux séismes dangereux.

tu.prop: Due to its inertness and isotopic signature, helium (the lightest noble gas) is a useful tracer for investigating the processes of storage and transfer of ﬂuids through the crust, including those prior to hazardous earthquakes.

We ﬁnd that low-magnitude earthquakes (M < 4) efﬁciently contribute to variations of the crustal helium output into the atmosphere which supports the impulsive nature of He degassing in tectonically active continental regions.

tuv.seg: Here we analyse a 12-year earthquake cat- alogue from the Irpinia Fault Zone, Italy, to compute the 4He outputs from the seismogenetic fault zones (from 104 to 106 mol y−1 with an annual tenfold variability) and compare these with estimates of long-term helium ﬂux.

Ici, nous analysons un catalogue sismique de 12 ans de la zone de faille d'Irpinia, en Italie, pour calculer les sorties 4He des zones de faille sismogénétiques (de 104 à 106 mol an−1 avec une variabilité annuelle décuplée) et les comparer avec des estimations du flux d'hélium à long terme.

tu.prop: Here we analyse a 12-year earthquake cat- alogue from the Irpinia Fault Zone, Italy, to compute the 4He outputs from the seismogenetic fault zones (from 104 to 106 mol y−1 with an annual tenfold variability) and compare these with estimates of long-term helium ﬂux.

We conclude that there is a quantitative relationship between crustal helium outputs and the volume of fault zones, and suggest variations in helium ﬂux may represent a gauge of changes in the stress ﬁeld that are related to the nucleation of earthquakes.

tuv.seg: We ﬁnd that low-magnitude earthquakes (M < 4) efﬁciently contribute to variations of the crustal helium output into the atmosphere which supports the impulsive nature of He degassing in tectonically active continental regions.

Nous constatons que les séismes de faible magnitude (M < 4) contribuent efficacement aux variations de la production d'hélium crustal dans l'atmosphère, ce qui soutient la nature impulsive du dégazage de l'hélium dans les régions continentales tectoniquement actives.

tu.prop: We ﬁnd that low-magnitude earthquakes (M < 4) efﬁciently contribute to variations of the crustal helium output into the atmosphere which supports the impulsive nature of He degassing in tectonically active continental regions.
1 Istituto Nazionale di Geoﬁsica e Vulcanologia, Sezione di Palermo, Palermo, Italy.

tuv.seg: We conclude that there is a quantitative relationship between crustal helium outputs and the volume of fault zones, and suggest variations in helium ﬂux may represent a gauge of changes in the stress ﬁeld that are related to the nucleation of earthquakes.

Nous concluons qu'il existe une relation quantitative entre les sorties d'hélium crustal et le volume des zones de faille, et suggérons que les variations du flux d'hélium peuvent représenter une jauge des changements dans le champ de contrainte qui sont liés à la nucléation des séismes.

tu.prop: We conclude that there is a quantitative relationship between crustal helium outputs and the volume of fault zones, and suggest variations in helium ﬂux may represent a gauge of changes in the stress ﬁeld that are related to the nucleation of earthquakes.
2 University of Naples Federico II, Naples, Italy.

Volcanic activity fed the release of huge amounts of volatiles into atmosphere2, making volcanoes major emitters of natural volatiles and hence controlling the budget of volatiles in the atmosphere.

tuv.seg: ARTICLE COMMUNICATIONS EARTH & ENVIRONMENT | https://doi.org/10.1038/s43247-022-00549-9 The composition of the atmosphere and its evolution over time was mainly driven by natural degassing from the Earth interior1 up to the industrial age.

ARTICLE COMMUNICATIONS EARTH & ENVIRONMENT | https://doi.org/10.1038/s43247-022-00549-9 La composition de l'atmosphère et son évolution dans le temps ont été principalement entraînées par le dégazage naturel de l'intérieur de la Terre1 jusqu'à l'âge industriel.

tu.prop: ARTICLE COMMUNICATIONS EARTH & ENVIRONMENT | https://doi.org/10.1038/s43247-022-00549-9 The composition of the atmosphere and its evolution over time was mainly driven by natural degassing from the Earth interior1 up to the industrial age.

Consider- able attention has recently been paid to the outgassing in active tectonic regions because faults are regions of enhanced perme- ability and porosity where ﬂuids can migrate through the whole crust3,4, and these latter can in turn alter the faults state of stress playing an active role in the generation of catastrophic earthquakes5–9.

tuv.seg: Volcanic activity fed the release of huge amounts of volatiles into atmosphere2, making volcanoes major emitters of natural volatiles and hence controlling the budget of volatiles in the atmosphere.

L'activité volcanique a permis la libération d'énormes quantités de substances volatiles dans l'atmosphère2, en faisant des volcans des émetteurs majeurs de substances volatiles naturelles et contrôlant ainsi le budget des substances volatiles dans l'atmosphère.

tu.prop: Volcanic activity fed the release of huge amounts of volatiles into atmosphere2, making volcanoes major emitters of natural volatiles and hence controlling the budget of volatiles in the atmosphere.
Noble gases are powerful tools for reconstructing the birth of the Earth, its interior and the evolution over time10–13.

tuv.seg: Consider- able attention has recently been paid to the outgassing in active tectonic regions because faults are regions of enhanced perme- ability and porosity where ﬂuids can migrate through the whole crust3,4, and these latter can in turn alter the faults state of stress playing an active role in the generation of catastrophic earthquakes5–9.

Récemment, une attention particulière a été accordée au dégazage dans les régions tectoniques actives, car les failles sont des régions de perméabilité et de porosité accrue où les fluides peuvent migrer à travers toute la croûte3,4, et ces derniers peuvent à leur tour modifier l'état de contrainte des failles jouant un rôle actif dans la génération de séismes catastrophiques5-9.

tu.prop: Consider- able attention has recently been paid to the outgassing in active tectonic regions because faults are regions of enhanced perme- ability and porosity where ﬂuids can migrate through the whole crust3,4, and these latter can in turn alter the faults state of stress playing an active role in the generation of catastrophic earthquakes5–9.
Their isotopic ratios are used to investigate the dynamics of natural processes such as volcanic eruptions and earthquakes14–18.

tuv.seg: Noble gases are powerful tools for reconstructing the birth of the Earth, its interior and the evolution over time10–13.
Les gaz nobles sont des outils puissants pour reconstruire la naissance de la Terre, son intérieur et son évolution dans le temps10-13.

tu.prop: Noble gases are powerful tools for reconstructing the birth of the Earth, its interior and the evolution over time10–13.
The lightest of the noble gases is helium (hereafter He), whose low atomic mass means that it is the only one that is able to escape into space10.

tuv.seg: Their isotopic ratios are used to investigate the dynamics of natural processes such as volcanic eruptions and earthquakes14–18.
Leurs rapports isotopiques sont utilisés pour étudier la dynamique des processus naturels tels que les éruptions volcaniques et les séismes14-18.

tu.prop: Their isotopic ratios are used to investigate the dynamics of natural processes such as volcanic eruptions and earthquakes14–18.

He on Earth is present as two isotopes, 3He and 4He, with the former being mainly primordial and stored in the mantle, and the latter continuously produced by uranium (U) and thorium (Th) decay in the Earth’s interior10.

tuv.seg: The lightest of the noble gases is helium (hereafter He), whose low atomic mass means that it is the only one that is able to escape into space10.
Le plus léger des gaz nobles est l'hélium (He), dont la faible masse atomique signifie qu'il est le seul à pouvoir fuir dans l'espace10.

tu.prop: The lightest of the noble gases is helium (hereafter He), whose low atomic mass means that it is the only one that is able to escape into space10.
The He ﬂux in stable continental regions is dominated by the radiogenic 4He that is produced in the crust (mantle He <1%)19.

tuv.seg: He on Earth is present as two isotopes, 3He and 4He, with the former being mainly primordial and stored in the mantle, and the latter continuously produced by uranium (U) and thorium (Th) decay in the Earth’s interior10.

L'hélium est présent sur Terre sous la forme de deux isotopes, 3He et 4He, le premier étant principalement primordial et stocké dans le manteau, et le second étant produit en continu par la désintégration de l'uranium (U) et du thorium (Th) à l'intérieur de la Terre10.

tu.prop: He on Earth is present as two isotopes, 3He and 4He, with the former being mainly primordial and stored in the mantle, and the latter continuously produced by uranium (U) and thorium (Th) decay in the Earth’s interior10.

In contrast, the primordial 3He escapes into the atmosphere mainly from volcanoes and in regions of active tectonic (from extensive to compressive), which makes the He isotopic ratio (3He/4He) a powerful tool for recognizing mantle- crust tectonics in the absence of other geological evidence4,19,20. tectonics23–27.

tuv.seg: The He ﬂux in stable continental regions is dominated by the radiogenic 4He that is produced in the crust (mantle He <1%)19.
Le flux de l'He dans les régions continentales stables est dominé par le 4He radiogénique qui est produit dans la croûte (He mantellique < 1 %)19.

tu.prop: The He ﬂux in stable continental regions is dominated by the radiogenic 4He that is produced in the crust (mantle He <1%)19.
The On the continents, groundwater acquires crustal 4He from the rocks that constitute the aquifer, and from the underlying crust during their circulation.

tuv.seg: In contrast, the primordial 3He escapes into the atmosphere mainly from volcanoes and in regions of active tectonic (from extensive to compressive), which makes the He isotopic ratio (3He/4He) a powerful tool for recognizing mantle- crust tectonics in the absence of other geological evidence4,19,20. tectonics23–27.

En revanche, le 3He primordial fuit dans l'atmosphère principalement des volcans et dans les régions de tectonique active (des régions extensives à compressives), ce qui fait du rapport isotopique He (3He/4He) un outil puissant pour reconnaître la tectonique de la croûte mantellique en l'absence d'autres preuves géologiques4,19,20.

tu.prop: In contrast, the primordial 3He escapes into the atmosphere mainly from volcanoes and in regions of active tectonic (from extensive to compressive), which makes the He isotopic ratio (3He/4He) a powerful tool for recognizing mantle- crust tectonics in the absence of other geological evidence4,19,20. tectonics23–27.
Indeed, the large aquifers worldwide 4He into contribute strongly to the discharge of crustal atmosphere21.

tuv.seg: The On the continents, groundwater acquires crustal 4He from the rocks that constitute the aquifer, and from the underlying crust during their circulation.
Sur les continents, les eaux souterraines acquièrent le 4He crustal à partir des roches qui constituent l'aquifère et de la croûte sous-jacente au cours de leur circulation.

tu.prop: The On the continents, groundwater acquires crustal 4He from the rocks that constitute the aquifer, and from the underlying crust during their circulation.

However, there is strong evidence that the con- tinental degassing is also episodic22, and depends on large-scale degassing metamorphism and mechanism implies large variabilities in time and space of the crustal 4He outgassing to the atmosphere and also enhanced mass transfer of ﬂuids through the crust22,27.

tuv.seg: Indeed, the large aquifers worldwide 4He into contribute strongly to the discharge of crustal atmosphere21.
En effet, les grands aquifères du monde entier contribuent fortement au déversement du 4He crustal dans l'atmosphère21.

tu.prop: Indeed, the large aquifers worldwide 4He into contribute strongly to the discharge of crustal atmosphere21.

It remains a challenge to quantify how the different processes occurring at depth (e.g. metamorphism, tectonics, earthquakes) sustain the release of crustal 4He and inﬂuence the impulsive nature of its degassing.

tuv.seg: However, there is strong evidence that the con- tinental degassing is also episodic22, and depends on large-scale degassing metamorphism and mechanism implies large variabilities in time and space of the crustal 4He outgassing to the atmosphere and also enhanced mass transfer of ﬂuids through the crust22,27.

Cependant, il existe des preuves solides que le dégazage continental est également épisodique22 et qu'il dépend d'un métamorphisme et d'un mécanisme de dégazage à grande échelle.Ce mécanisme de dégazage implique de grandes variations dans le temps et dans l'espace du dégazage crustal du 4He dans l'atmosphère et également un transfert de masse accru des fluides à travers la croûte22,27.

tu.prop: However, there is strong evidence that the con- tinental degassing is also episodic22, and depends on large-scale degassing metamorphism and mechanism implies large variabilities in time and space of the crustal 4He outgassing to the atmosphere and also enhanced mass transfer of ﬂuids through the crust22,27.

In this scenario, identifying quantitative constraints of the volume of the fault zone at depth and the related capacity of rock underlying the impulsive degassing of 4He would provide powerful tools for understanding Earth degassing and the natural processes that are associated with disastrous natural events such as earthquakes. latter The enhancement of rock deformation due to tectonics results in the opening and development of fractures within the crust at the grain scale.

tuv.seg: It remains a challenge to quantify how the different processes occurring at depth (e.g. metamorphism, tectonics, earthquakes) sustain the release of crustal 4He and inﬂuence the impulsive nature of its degassing.

Il reste difficile de quantifier comment les différents processus qui se produisent en profondeur (par exemple, métamorphisme, tectonique, séismes) soutiennent la libération du 4He crustal et influencent la nature impulsive de son dégazage.

tu.prop: It remains a challenge to quantify how the different processes occurring at depth (e.g. metamorphism, tectonics, earthquakes) sustain the release of crustal 4He and inﬂuence the impulsive nature of its degassing.
Such microscale fracturation increases as an effect of dilatation until macroscopic failures occur and the strain is released.

tuv.seg: In this scenario, identifying quantitative constraints of the volume of the fault zone at depth and the related capacity of rock underlying the impulsive degassing of 4He would provide powerful tools for understanding Earth degassing and the natural processes that are associated with disastrous natural events such as earthquakes. latter The enhancement of rock deformation due to tectonics results in the opening and development of fractures within the crust at the grain scale.

Dans ce scénario, l'identification des contraintes quantitatives du volume de la zone de faille en profondeur et de la capacité connexe des roches sous-jacentes au dégazage impulsif du 4He fournirait des outils puissants pour comprendre le dégazage de la Terre et les processus naturels associés aux catastrophes naturelles telles que les séismes. L'accroissement des déformations rocheuses causées par la tectonique donne lieu à l'ouverture et au développement des fractures dans la croûte à l'échelle du grain.

tu.prop: In this scenario, identifying quantitative constraints of the volume of the fault zone at depth and the related capacity of rock underlying the impulsive degassing of 4He would provide powerful tools for understanding Earth degassing and the natural processes that are associated with disastrous natural events such as earthquakes. latter The enhancement of rock deformation due to tectonics results in the opening and development of fractures within the crust at the grain scale.

As a consequence of the dilatancy-related microscale fracturation, crustal 4He stored in rocks is liberated faster from minerals and rocks25,26,28 and it escapes towards the pore ﬂuids and successively to the atmosphere23. through the crust framework, earthquake-related changes in volumetric strains increase the release of crustal 4He from rocks27, highlighting the direct linkage between seismicity and the impulsive nature of the crustal 4He degassing.

tuv.seg: Such microscale fracturation increases as an effect of dilatation until macroscopic failures occur and the strain is released.
Cette fracturation à l'échelle microscopique augmente en raison de la dilatation jusqu'à ce que des ruptures à l'échelle macroscopiques se produisent et que la tension soit relâchée.

tu.prop: Such microscale fracturation increases as an effect of dilatation until macroscopic failures occur and the strain is released.

This evidence is augmented by rare observations of increases in the amounts of crustal 4He in natural ﬂuids associated with high- magnitude earthquakes, such as the Kumamoto Earthquake in 2016 (M = 7.3) and the Kobe earthquake in 1995 (M = 7.2)29,30.

tuv.seg: As a consequence of the dilatancy-related microscale fracturation, crustal 4He stored in rocks is liberated faster from minerals and rocks25,26,28 and it escapes towards the pore ﬂuids and successively to the atmosphere23. through the crust framework, earthquake-related changes in volumetric strains increase the release of crustal 4He from rocks27, highlighting the direct linkage between seismicity and the impulsive nature of the crustal 4He degassing.

En conséquence de la fracturation à l'échelle microscopique liée à la dilatation, le 4He crustal stocké dans les roches est libéré plus rapidement des minéraux et des roches25,26,28, et il s'échappe vers les fluides poreux et successivement à travers la croûte jusqu'à l'atmosphère23. Dans ce cadre, les changements de déformations volumétriques liés aux séismes augmentent la libération de 4He crustal des roches27, soulignant le lien direct entre la sismicité et la nature impulsive du dégazage du 4He crustal.

tu.prop: As a consequence of the dilatancy-related microscale fracturation, crustal 4He stored in rocks is liberated faster from minerals and rocks25,26,28 and it escapes towards the pore ﬂuids and successively to the atmosphere23. through the crust framework, earthquake-related changes in volumetric strains increase the release of crustal 4He from rocks27, highlighting the direct linkage between seismicity and the impulsive nature of the crustal 4He degassing.
In this While brittle faults are often imagined as single planar struc- tures, in reality they should be seen as complex volumetric fault zones composed of a variety of internal structures.

tuv.seg: This evidence is augmented by rare observations of increases in the amounts of crustal 4He in natural ﬂuids associated with high- magnitude earthquakes, such as the Kumamoto Earthquake in 2016 (M = 7.3) and the Kobe earthquake in 1995 (M = 7.2)29,30.

Cette preuve est appuyée par de rares observations d'augmentation des quantités de 4He crustal dans les fluides naturels associés aux séismes de forte intensité, tels que les séismes de Kumamoto en 2016 (M=7.3) et celui de Kobe en 1995 (M=7.2)29,30.

tu.prop: This evidence is augmented by rare observations of increases in the amounts of crustal 4He in natural ﬂuids associated with high- magnitude earthquakes, such as the Kumamoto Earthquake in 2016 (M = 7.3) and the Kobe earthquake in 1995 (M = 7.2)29,30.
A fault zone can be schematically simpliﬁed in two main structural regions (Fig. 1): (1) the fault core and (2) the damage zone31.

tuv.seg: In this While brittle faults are often imagined as single planar struc- tures, in reality they should be seen as complex volumetric fault zones composed of a variety of internal structures.

Tandis que les failles cassantes sont souvent représentées comme étant de simples structures planes, elles devraient en réalité être vues comme des zones de faille volumétriques complexes composées d'une variété de structures internes.

tu.prop: In this While brittle faults are often imagined as single planar struc- tures, in reality they should be seen as complex volumetric fault zones composed of a variety of internal structures.
The fault the fault displacement core is referred to the volume of highly localized strain and intense shearing where most of is accommodated by structures as gouges, cataclasites and breccias.

tuv.seg: A fault zone can be schematically simpliﬁed in two main structural regions (Fig. 1): (1) the fault core and (2) the damage zone31.
Une zone de faille peut être simplement schématisée avec une structure en deux parties principales (Fig. 1) : (1) le cœur de la faille et (2) la zone endommagée31.

tu.prop: A fault zone can be schematically simpliﬁed in two main structural regions (Fig. 1): (1) the fault core and (2) the damage zone31.

A damage zone is the broader volume around the fault core that exhibits second-order structures (e.g., subsidiary faults, fractures, and veins), which long-term evolution represents a key factor for strain distribution, earthquake rupture propagation, and ﬂuid circulation through the crust.

tuv.seg: The fault the fault displacement core is referred to the volume of highly localized strain and intense shearing where most of is accommodated by structures as gouges, cataclasites and breccias.

Le cœur de la faille correspond au volume d'une déformation très localisée et de cisaillement intense où la majeure partie du glissement de la faille est localisée dans des formations telles que des gouges, des cataclasites et des brèches.

tu.prop: The fault the fault displacement core is referred to the volume of highly localized strain and intense shearing where most of is accommodated by structures as gouges, cataclasites and breccias.
These regions represent the primary crustal volumes contributing to the enhanced release of crustal 4He.

tuv.seg: A damage zone is the broader volume around the fault core that exhibits second-order structures (e.g., subsidiary faults, fractures, and veins), which long-term evolution represents a key factor for strain distribution, earthquake rupture propagation, and ﬂuid circulation through the crust.

Une zone endommagée correspond au volume plus large autour du cœur de la faille, à partir de laquelle apparaissent des formations de second ordre (par exemple, des failles secondaires, des fractures ainsi que des veines de faille) et dont l'évolution à long terme représente un élément clé dans la distribution des contraintes, dans la propagation de la rupture des séismes ainsi que dans la circulation des fluides à travers la croûte.

tu.prop: A damage zone is the broader volume around the fault core that exhibits second-order structures (e.g., subsidiary faults, fractures, and veins), which long-term evolution represents a key factor for strain distribution, earthquake rupture propagation, and ﬂuid circulation through the crust.

Its release from the rock increases with fracture develop- ment, with the 4He output reaching up to 104 times the crustal 4He steady-state ﬂux23,32 that mainly dominates 4He degassing in old stable continental regions22.

tuv.seg: These regions represent the primary crustal volumes contributing to the enhanced release of crustal 4He.
Ces zones correspondent aux principaux volumes crustaux contribuant à l'augmentation de la libération du 4He crustal.

tu.prop: These regions represent the primary crustal volumes contributing to the enhanced release of crustal 4He.

In this framework, it must be considered that both the damage zone and the fault core can host large amounts of ﬂuids of different origins and compositions (e.g. H2O, CO2)33,34, which play a fun- damental role as a carrier of 4He that migrates through the crust.

tuv.seg: Its release from the rock increases with fracture develop- ment, with the 4He output reaching up to 104 times the crustal 4He steady-state ﬂux23,32 that mainly dominates 4He degassing in old stable continental regions22.

Sa libération à partir de la roche augmente avec l'ouverture des fractures, avec un volume de 4He dégazé jusqu'à 104 fois supérieur au flux de 4He crustal en régime permanent23,32, dominant principalement le dégazage de 4He dans d'anciennes zones continentales stables22.

tu.prop: Its release from the rock increases with fracture develop- ment, with the 4He output reaching up to 104 times the crustal 4He steady-state ﬂux23,32 that mainly dominates 4He degassing in old stable continental regions22.

In a conceptual model (Fig. 1) where deformation and the related seismicity can result in 4He escaping from the minerals and rocks in which it was produced and accumulated, the fault zones thus represent (1) the zone of enhanced release of crustal 4He and (2) the networks of pathways through which ﬂuids preferentially transfer crustal 4He through the crust towards the atmosphere (Fig. 1).

tuv.seg: In this framework, it must be considered that both the damage zone and the fault core can host large amounts of ﬂuids of different origins and compositions (e.g. H2O, CO2)33,34, which play a fun- damental role as a carrier of 4He that migrates through the crust.

Dans ce cadre, il faut tenir compte du fait que la zone endommagée et le cœur de faille peuvent contenir une grande quantité de fluides d'origines et de compositions différentes (e.g H20, CO2)33,34, qui jouent un rôle fondamental dans la migration du 4He à travers la croûte.

tu.prop: In this framework, it must be considered that both the damage zone and the fault core can host large amounts of ﬂuids of different origins and compositions (e.g. H2O, CO2)33,34, which play a fun- damental role as a carrier of 4He that migrates through the crust.

Several ﬁeldwork and laboratory studies have investigated the volumetric changes and evolution of the fault core and damage zone due to tectonics31,35, as well as the relationship between the nature of the damage zone and earthquake characteristics36,37.

tuv.seg: In a conceptual model (Fig. 1) where deformation and the related seismicity can result in 4He escaping from the minerals and rocks in which it was produced and accumulated, the fault zones thus represent (1) the zone of enhanced release of crustal 4He and (2) the networks of pathways through which ﬂuids preferentially transfer crustal 4He through the crust towards the atmosphere (Fig. 1).

Dans le modèle conceptuel (Fig. 1) où la déformation et la sismicité associée peuvent engendrer la libération du 4He des minéraux et des roches dans lesquels il était produit et accumulé, les zones de failles représentent alors (1) l'endroit où le dégazage du 4He crustal est important et (2) des voies privilégiées au travers desquelles les fluides transfèrent principalement le 4He crustal vers l'atmosphère (Fig. 1).

tu.prop: In a conceptual model (Fig. 1) where deformation and the related seismicity can result in 4He escaping from the minerals and rocks in which it was produced and accumulated, the fault zones thus represent (1) the zone of enhanced release of crustal 4He and (2) the networks of pathways through which ﬂuids preferentially transfer crustal 4He through the crust towards the atmosphere (Fig. 1).
However, damage zone width is generally deﬁned by studies of exhumed structures.

tuv.seg: Several ﬁeldwork and laboratory studies have investigated the volumetric changes and evolution of the fault core and damage zone due to tectonics31,35, as well as the relationship between the nature of the damage zone and earthquake characteristics36,37.

Plusieurs études de terrain et études en laboratoire ont exploré les changements volumétriques et l'évolution du cœur de faille et de la zone endommagée causés par la tectonique31,35, ainsi que la relation entre la nature de la zone endommagée et les caractéristiques du séisme36,37.

tu.prop: Several ﬁeldwork and laboratory studies have investigated the volumetric changes and evolution of the fault core and damage zone due to tectonics31,35, as well as the relationship between the nature of the damage zone and earthquake characteristics36,37.

Inconsistencies in calculation of damage zone width (e.g., due to subjectivity or ambiguity of deﬁnition and measures) makes it difﬁcult to assess the volume of rocks at depth associated with faults where crustal 4He release is enhanced.

tuv.seg: However, damage zone width is generally deﬁned by studies of exhumed structures.
Cependant, la largeur de la zone endommagée est généralement définie par les études des structures exhumées.

tu.prop: However, damage zone width is generally deﬁned by studies of exhumed structures.
Microearthquakes can revo- lutionize our approach to the study of crustal phenomena.

tuv.seg: Inconsistencies in calculation of damage zone width (e.g., due to subjectivity or ambiguity of deﬁnition and measures) makes it difﬁcult to assess the volume of rocks at depth associated with faults where crustal 4He release is enhanced.

Les incohérences dans le calcul de la largeur de la zone endommagée (e.g., à cause de la subjectivité ou de l'ambiguïté de la définition et des mesures) rendent difficile l'estimation du volume des roches en profondeur associées aux failles où le dégazage du 4He crustal est accru.

tu.prop: Inconsistencies in calculation of damage zone width (e.g., due to subjectivity or ambiguity of deﬁnition and measures) makes it difﬁcult to assess the volume of rocks at depth associated with faults where crustal 4He release is enhanced.

In recent decades the development of dense seismic networks worldwide has generated rich, long-term earthquake catalogues, and micro- earthquakes can now be used as beacons for observing crustal processes down to tens of kilometres depth. to inquire about Microearthquakes indeed occur on damage zones and, hence, they allow determining the detailed structure of seismogenic fault zones at depth, the relationships between earthquake nucleation, rock types and the presence of ﬂuids in the fault zone38.

tuv.seg: Microearthquakes can revo- lutionize our approach to the study of crustal phenomena.
Les microséismes peuvent révolutionner notre approche de l'étude des phénomènes crustaux.

tu.prop: Microearthquakes can revo- lutionize our approach to the study of crustal phenomena.

The outcomes of such analyses provide new opportunities to decipher the effects of seismicity on Earth degassing and provide fundamental new insight for reconciling the dichotomy between the steady-state model and the impulsive nature of Earth degassing.

tuv.seg: In recent decades the development of dense seismic networks worldwide has generated rich, long-term earthquake catalogues, and micro- earthquakes can now be used as beacons for observing crustal processes down to tens of kilometres depth. to inquire about Microearthquakes indeed occur on damage zones and, hence, they allow determining the detailed structure of seismogenic fault zones at depth, the relationships between earthquake nucleation, rock types and the presence of ﬂuids in the fault zone38.

Au cours des dernières décennies, le développement de réseaux sismiques denses dans le monde entier a produit de riches registres de séismes à long terme, et les microséismes peuvent maintenant être utilisés comme balises pour observer les processus crustaux jusqu'à des dizaines de kilomètres de profondeur pour se renseigner sur les microséismes qui se produisent en effet sur les zones endommagées. Par conséquent, ils permettent ainsi de déterminer la structure détaillée des zones de faille sismogènes en profondeur, les relations entre la nucléation des séismes, les types de roches et la présence de fluides dans la zone de faille38.

tu.prop: In recent decades the development of dense seismic networks worldwide has generated rich, long-term earthquake catalogues, and micro- earthquakes can now be used as beacons for observing crustal processes down to tens of kilometres depth. to inquire about Microearthquakes indeed occur on damage zones and, hence, they allow determining the detailed structure of seismogenic fault zones at depth, the relationships between earthquake nucleation, rock types and the presence of ﬂuids in the fault zone38.

Results and discussion Here, we exploit the role of low magnitude earthquakes (M < 4) on He degassing in continental regions by computing the variability of the crustal 4He output over time for the seismogenic volumes of rock associated to a 12-year-long earthquake catalogue.

tuv.seg: The outcomes of such analyses provide new opportunities to decipher the effects of seismicity on Earth degassing and provide fundamental new insight for reconciling the dichotomy between the steady-state model and the impulsive nature of Earth degassing.

Le résultat de telles analyses apportent de nouvelles opportunités de décrypter les effets de la sismicité sur le dégazage de la Terre, et fournissent de nouvelles perspectives fondamentales pour réconcilier la dichotomie entre le modèle de l'état stationnaire et la nature impulsive du dégazage de la Terre.

tu.prop: The outcomes of such analyses provide new opportunities to decipher the effects of seismicity on Earth degassing and provide fundamental new insight for reconciling the dichotomy between the steady-state model and the impulsive nature of Earth degassing.

This study considered the Irpinia fault zone (hereafter IRPZ) in central-southern Apennine (Italy) (Fig. 2), a region affected by major deformational processes in terms of active displacements and seismogenic processes that have led to high-magnitude dis- astrous earthquakes (i.e. M = 6.4 in 1561, M = 6.9 in 1694, M = 5.7 in 1826, M = 5.9 in 1853, M = 7.0 in 1857), with the most recent being the M = 6.9 Irpinia earthquake in 198039, which occurred along NW-SE trending normal faults.

tuv.seg: Results and discussion Here, we exploit the role of low magnitude earthquakes (M < 4) on He degassing in continental regions by computing the variability of the crustal 4He output over time for the seismogenic volumes of rock associated to a 12-year-long earthquake catalogue.

Résultats et discussion Ici, nous exploitons le rôle des séismes de faible magnitude (M < 4) sur le dégazage de He dans les régions continentales en calculant la variabilité de la production crustale de 4He au fil du temps pour les volumes sismogènes de roche associés à un registre de séismes sur 12 ans.

tu.prop: Results and discussion Here, we exploit the role of low magnitude earthquakes (M < 4) on He degassing in continental regions by computing the variability of the crustal 4He output over time for the seismogenic volumes of rock associated to a 12-year-long earthquake catalogue.
This latter large event occurred as a complex rupture process involving multiple segments (mostly with dip ~60° eastward)39.

tuv.seg: This study considered the Irpinia fault zone (hereafter IRPZ) in central-southern Apennine (Italy) (Fig. 2), a region affected by major deformational processes in terms of active displacements and seismogenic processes that have led to high-magnitude dis- astrous earthquakes (i.e. M = 6.4 in 1561, M = 6.9 in 1694, M = 5.7 in 1826, M = 5.9 in 1853, M = 7.0 in 1857), with the most recent being the M = 6.9 Irpinia earthquake in 198039, which occurred along NW-SE trending normal faults.

Cette étude a examiné la zone de faille d'Irpinia (désignée ci-après par IRPZ) dans le centre-sud de l'Apennin (Italie) (Fig. 2), une région affectée par des processus de déformation majeurs en termes de déplacements actifs et de processus sismogéniques qui ont conduit à des séismes de haute magnitude catastrophiques (c'est-à-dire M = 6,4 en 1561, M = 6,9 en 1694, M = 5,7 en 1826, M = 5,9 en 1853, M = 7,0 en 1857), le plus récent étant le séisme d'Irpinia de M = 6,9 qui s'est produit en 198039, le long de failles normales orientées NO-SE.

tu.prop: This study considered the Irpinia fault zone (hereafter IRPZ) in central-southern Apennine (Italy) (Fig. 2), a region affected by major deformational processes in terms of active displacements and seismogenic processes that have led to high-magnitude dis- astrous earthquakes (i.e. M = 6.4 in 1561, M = 6.9 in 1694, M = 5.7 in 1826, M = 5.9 in 1853, M = 7.0 in 1857), with the most recent being the M = 6.9 Irpinia earthquake in 198039, which occurred along NW-SE trending normal faults.

tuv.seg: This latter large event occurred as a complex rupture process involving multiple segments (mostly with dip ~60° eastward)39.
Ce dernier événement important s'est produit sous la forme d'un processus de rupture complexe impliquant plusieurs segments (principalement avec un pendage ~60° vers l'est) 39.

tu.prop: This latter large event occurred as a complex rupture process involving multiple segments (mostly with dip ~60° eastward)39.

En raison de la tectonique40 et de l'apparition de séisme de forte complexité, l'IRPZ peut être considérée comme l'une des zones sismiques les plus dangereuses de la région méditerranéenne.

Furthermore, the volumes of rocks that constitute the faults zones (damage zone plus fault core) are extensively deformed and fractured enhancing the release of crustal 4He because of the volumetric stress change.

tuv.seg: In active tectonic regions, the faults are region of enhanced porosity and permeability and they are the network of pathways through which ﬂuids preferentially transfer to the atmosphere.

Dans les régions tectoniques actives, les failles sont une région de porosité et de perméabilité plus prononcées et elles sont le réseau de voies à travers lesquelles les fluides se transfèrent préférentiellement dans l'atmosphère.

tu.prop: In active tectonic regions, the faults are region of enhanced porosity and permeability and they are the network of pathways through which ﬂuids preferentially transfer to the atmosphere.
The insert shows a zoomed schematic representation of the fault zones as fault core plus the damage zone and the adjacent un-deformed rocks.

tuv.seg: Furthermore, the volumes of rocks that constitute the faults zones (damage zone plus fault core) are extensively deformed and fractured enhancing the release of crustal 4He because of the volumetric stress change.

De plus, les volumes de roches qui constituent les zones de failles (zone endommagée plus noyau de faille) sont largement déformés et fracturés, améliorant la libération de 4He crustal en raison du changement de contrainte volumétrique.

tu.prop: Furthermore, the volumes of rocks that constitute the faults zones (damage zone plus fault core) are extensively deformed and fractured enhancing the release of crustal 4He because of the volumetric stress change.

During the last 10 years, seismicity in the IRPZ has been monitored in real time by the Irpinia Near Fault Observatory which (https://www.epos-eu.org/tcs/near-fault-observatories), also includes the Irpinia Seismic Network (ISNet; http://isnet. ﬁsica.unina.it).

L'insert montre une représentation schématique agrandie des zones de faille en tant que noyau de faille, ainsi que la zone endommagée et les roches adjacentes non déformées.

The ISNet represents a natural laboratory for studying fault evolution and rupture processes.

tuv.seg: During the last 10 years, seismicity in the IRPZ has been monitored in real time by the Irpinia Near Fault Observatory which (https://www.epos-eu.org/tcs/near-fault-observatories), also includes the Irpinia Seismic Network (ISNet; http://isnet. ﬁsica.unina.it).

Au cours des 10 dernières années, la sismicité dans l'IRPZ a été surveillée en temps réel par l'Irpinia Near Fault Observatory (https://www.epos-eu.org/tcs/near-fault-observatories), qui comprend également l'Irpinia Seismic Network (ISNet ; http ://isnet. ﬁsica.unina.it).

tu.prop: During the last 10 years, seismicity in the IRPZ has been monitored in real time by the Irpinia Near Fault Observatory which (https://www.epos-eu.org/tcs/near-fault-observatories), also includes the Irpinia Seismic Network (ISNet; http://isnet. ﬁsica.unina.it).

tuv.seg: The ISNet represents a natural laboratory for studying fault evolution and rupture processes.
L'ISNet se présente comme un laboratoire naturel pour l'étude de l'évolution des failles et des processus de rupture.

tu.prop: The ISNet represents a natural laboratory for studying fault evolution and rupture processes.

tuv.seg: In this study we analysed a 12-year-long earthquake catalogue (from 2008 to 2019) of distributed seismicity in an area of approximately 3700 km2 (ca.
Dans cette étude, nous avons analysé un registre de séismes sur 12 ans (de 2008 à 2019) de sismicité distribuée dans une zone d'environ 3700 km2 (env.

tu.prop: In this study we analysed a 12-year-long earthquake catalogue (from 2008 to 2019) of distributed seismicity in an area of approximately 3700 km2 (ca.
The larger magnitude events during this 12-year-period have ranged from M = 3.4 (in 2009) to M = 4.4 (in 2012 and 2019).

The hypocentres of the present-day earthquakes appear to be spread over a large crustal volume around the fault segments that generated the M = 6.9 Irpinia earthquake in 1980, spanning depths from 2 to 20 km (Fig. 2), with a higher density of events at around 7 km and 12 km in carbonate lithology (Fig. 2).

tuv.seg: The larger magnitude events during this 12-year-period have ranged from M = 3.4 (in 2009) to M = 4.4 (in 2012 and 2019).
Les événements de plus grande ampleur au cours de cette période de 12 ans ont varié de M = 3,4 (en 2009) à M = 4,4 (en 2012 et 2019).

tu.prop: The larger magnitude events during this 12-year-period have ranged from M = 3.4 (in 2009) to M = 4.4 (in 2012 and 2019).
This sector of central-southern Apennine that coincides with the IRPZ is characterized by the outgassing of volatiles of deep origin, and CO2 is the dominant gaseous species7,41,42.

tuv.seg: The hypocentres of the present-day earthquakes appear to be spread over a large crustal volume around the fault segments that generated the M = 6.9 Irpinia earthquake in 1980, spanning depths from 2 to 20 km (Fig. 2), with a higher density of events at around 7 km and 12 km in carbonate lithology (Fig. 2).

Les hypocentres des séismes actuels semblent être répartis sur un grand volume crustal autour des segments de faille qui ont généré le séisme d'Irpinia M = 6,9 en 1980, couvrant des profondeurs de 2 à 20 km (Fig. 2), avec une densité d'événements plus élevée à environ 7 km et 12 km en lithologie carbonatée (Fig. 2).

tu.prop: The hypocentres of the present-day earthquakes appear to be spread over a large crustal volume around the fault segments that generated the M = 6.9 Irpinia earthquake in 1980, spanning depths from 2 to 20 km (Fig. 2), with a higher density of events at around 7 km and 12 km in carbonate lithology (Fig. 2).

Not- withstanding that the region is far from active volcanism (>70 km), the He isotopic ratio (3He/4He) in the high-ﬂux CO2 emissions is extremely variable, and peaks at 2.9 Ra (Ra is the 3He/4He value in the atmosphere)42–44.

tuv.seg: This sector of central-southern Apennine that coincides with the IRPZ is characterized by the outgassing of volatiles of deep origin, and CO2 is the dominant gaseous species7,41,42.
Ce secteur au centre-sud des Apennins, qui coïncide avec l'IRPZ, est caractérisé par le dégazage de volatiles d'origine profonde, parmi lesquels le CO2 est l'espèce gazeuse dominante7,41,42.

tu.prop: This sector of central-southern Apennine that coincides with the IRPZ is characterized by the outgassing of volatiles of deep origin, and CO2 is the dominant gaseous species7,41,42.
The contribution of the signature (6.3 Ra for subcontinental air-derived He is negligible in all these gas emissions in IRPZ42,43.

tuv.seg: Not- withstanding that the region is far from active volcanism (>70 km), the He isotopic ratio (3He/4He) in the high-ﬂux CO2 emissions is extremely variable, and peaks at 2.9 Ra (Ra is the 3He/4He value in the atmosphere)42–44.

Bien que la région soit éloignée des zones de volcanisme actif (>70 km), le rapport isotopique d'He (3He/4He) dans les émissions de CO2 à haut flux est extrêmement variable et culmine à 2,9 Ra (Ra est la valeur de 3He/4He dans l'atmosphère) 42-44.

tu.prop: Not- withstanding that the region is far from active volcanism (>70 km), the He isotopic ratio (3He/4He) in the high-ﬂux CO2 emissions is extremely variable, and peaks at 2.9 Ra (Ra is the 3He/4He value in the atmosphere)42–44.

This peak value (2.9 Ra) is lower than the typical mantle He lithospheric isotopic mantle)45 and is markedly higher than the typical crustal radio- genic signature (0.01–0.05 Ra)46, which clearly indicates the presence of mantle-derived He in the IRPZ that is diluted by variable contributions of crust-derived 4He.

tuv.seg: The contribution of the signature (6.3 Ra for subcontinental air-derived He is negligible in all these gas emissions in IRPZ42,43.
La contribution de l'He dérivé de l'air est négligeable dans toutes ces émissions de gaz dans l'IRPZ42,43.

tu.prop: The contribution of the signature (6.3 Ra for subcontinental air-derived He is negligible in all these gas emissions in IRPZ42,43.

Furthermore, high crustal radiogenic 4He outputs have also been computed for the IRPZ (up to 3.74 × 104 mol y−1)46 that cannot be explained using the steady-state model of the whole-crust production and release, the latter being up to 4 orders of magnitude lower46.

tuv.seg: This peak value (2.9 Ra) is lower than the typical mantle He lithospheric isotopic mantle)45 and is markedly higher than the typical crustal radio- genic signature (0.01–0.05 Ra)46, which clearly indicates the presence of mantle-derived He in the IRPZ that is diluted by variable contributions of crust-derived 4He.

Cette valeur maximale (2,9 Ra) est inférieure à la signature isotopique typique de l'He (6,3 Ra pour le manteau lithosphérique sous-continental) 45 et est nettement supérieure à la signature radiogénique caractéristique de la croûte (0,01-0,05 Ra) 46, ce qui indique clairement la présence d'He dérivé du manteau dans l'IRPZ qui est dilué par des contributions variables de 4He dérivé de la croûte.

tu.prop: This peak value (2.9 Ra) is lower than the typical mantle He lithospheric isotopic mantle)45 and is markedly higher than the typical crustal radio- genic signature (0.01–0.05 Ra)46, which clearly indicates the presence of mantle-derived He in the IRPZ that is diluted by variable contributions of crust-derived 4He.

This evi- dence clearly implies that tectonics control the transfer of the volatiles in this seismically active sector of central-southern Italy42,43,46, where they play an active role in regional seismicity8, making this investigating the relationship between earthquakes and He degassing.

tuv.seg: Furthermore, high crustal radiogenic 4He outputs have also been computed for the IRPZ (up to 3.74 × 104 mol y−1)46 that cannot be explained using the steady-state model of the whole-crust production and release, the latter being up to 4 orders of magnitude lower46.

De plus, des émissions élevées de 4He radiogénique ont également été estimées pour l'IRPZ (jusqu'à 3,74 x 104 mol y-1)46 et elles ne peuvent être expliquées en utilisant le modèle en régime permanent de la production ainsi que de la libération crustale dans sa totalité, cette dernière étant jusqu'à 4 ordre de grandeur plus faible46.

tu.prop: Furthermore, high crustal radiogenic 4He outputs have also been computed for the IRPZ (up to 3.74 × 104 mol y−1)46 that cannot be explained using the steady-state model of the whole-crust production and release, the latter being up to 4 orders of magnitude lower46.

Furthermore, the He isotopic signatures in natural ﬂuids here have been constant over about 20 years of observations, indicating that the mixing of mantle-crust ﬂuids is not perturbed during long inter-seismic period46 (Supplementary Note 5). region ideal At the global scale, the crustal 4He ﬂux from continental regions is 3 × 1010 atoms m–2 s–1, with a variability of a factor 2X22.

tuv.seg: This evi- dence clearly implies that tectonics control the transfer of the volatiles in this seismically active sector of central-southern Italy42,43,46, where they play an active role in regional seismicity8, making this investigating the relationship between earthquakes and He degassing.

Cette preuve démontre clairement que la tectonique contrôle le transfert des gaz volatiles dans ce secteur sismiquement actif du centre-sud de l'Italie42,43,46. Ces gaz jouent un rôle actif dans la sismicité régionale8, ce qui rend cette région idéale pour étudier la relation entre les séismes et le dégazage de l'He.

tu.prop: This evi- dence clearly implies that tectonics control the transfer of the volatiles in this seismically active sector of central-southern Italy42,43,46, where they play an active role in regional seismicity8, making this investigating the relationship between earthquakes and He degassing.
We computed the year-by-year steady-state degassing in the IRPZ for COMMUNICATIONS EARTH & ENVIRONMENT | (2022) 3:224 | https://doi.org/10.1038/s43247-022-00549-9 | www.nature.com/commsenv 3

tuv.seg: Furthermore, the He isotopic signatures in natural ﬂuids here have been constant over about 20 years of observations, indicating that the mixing of mantle-crust ﬂuids is not perturbed during long inter-seismic period46 (Supplementary Note 5). region ideal At the global scale, the crustal 4He ﬂux from continental regions is 3 × 1010 atoms m–2 s–1, with a variability of a factor 2X22.

En outre, les signatures isotopiques de l'He dans les fluides naturels de cette région ont été constantes pendant environ 20 ans d'observations, ce qui indique que le mélange des fluides mantelliques et crustaux n'est pas perturbé pendant les périodes inter-sismiques longues46 (Note supplémentaire 5). À l'échelle mondiale, le flux crustal de 4He des régions continentales est de 3 × 1010 atomes m–2 s–1, avec une variabilité d'un facteur 2X22.

tu.prop: Furthermore, the He isotopic signatures in natural ﬂuids here have been constant over about 20 years of observations, indicating that the mixing of mantle-crust ﬂuids is not perturbed during long inter-seismic period46 (Supplementary Note 5). region ideal At the global scale, the crustal 4He ﬂux from continental regions is 3 × 1010 atoms m–2 s–1, with a variability of a factor 2X22.

The corre- sponding annual output from the whole crust in the IRPZ ranges from 1.2 × 103 to 4.8 × 103 mol y−1 (Supplementary Table 3). to 2.1 × 1010 atoms m–2 We also focused on faults that nucleate earthquakes in the IRPZ.

tuv.seg: The annual crustal steady-state 4He ﬂux in the IRPZ ranges from s–1, where the highest 8.7 × 109 the worldwide continental ﬂux value overlaps the range of (3 × 1010 atoms m–2 s–1, with a twofold variability)22.

Le flux annuel de 4He à l'état stable de la croûte dans l'IRPZ varie de 8.7 × 109 à 2.1 × 1010 atoms m–2 s–1, où la valeur la plus élevée chevauche la plage du flux continental mondial (3 × 1010 atomes m–2 s–1, avec une double variabilité)22.

tu.prop: The annual crustal steady-state 4He ﬂux in the IRPZ ranges from s–1, where the highest 8.7 × 109 the worldwide continental ﬂux value overlaps the range of (3 × 1010 atoms m–2 s–1, with a twofold variability)22.

We computed the volumes of both the fault cores and the damage zones of the seismogenic faults, starting from the cata- logue of the earthquake source parameters obtained34 for the ~2300 earthquakes that occurred during 2008–2019 (Supple- mentary Note 3 and 4 and Supplementary Data 1).

tuv.seg: The corre- sponding annual output from the whole crust in the IRPZ ranges from 1.2 × 103 to 4.8 × 103 mol y−1 (Supplementary Table 3). to 2.1 × 1010 atoms m–2 We also focused on faults that nucleate earthquakes in the IRPZ.

La production annuelle correspondante de la croûte entière dans l'IRPZ varie de 1,2 × 103 à 4,8 × 103 mol y−1 (tableau supplémentaire 3). Nous nous sommes également concentrés sur les failles qui nucléent les tremblements de terre dans l'IRPZ.

tu.prop: The corre- sponding annual output from the whole crust in the IRPZ ranges from 1.2 × 103 to 4.8 × 103 mol y−1 (Supplementary Table 3). to 2.1 × 1010 atoms m–2 We also focused on faults that nucleate earthquakes in the IRPZ.

The volume of the fault core (Vfc) was calculated for each event, and then the annual sum was computed (Supplementary note 3), which yielded Vfc values ranging from 4.4 × 10–8 km3 (sum of Vfc in 2008) to 1.9 × 10–6 km3 (sum of Vfc in 2019).

tuv.seg: We computed the volumes of both the fault cores and the damage zones of the seismogenic faults, starting from the cata- logue of the earthquake source parameters obtained34 for the ~2300 earthquakes that occurred during 2008–2019 (Supple- mentary Note 3 and 4 and Supplementary Data 1).

Nous avons calculé les volumes des carottes de faille et des zones endommagées des failles sismogènes, à partir du catalogue des paramètres de la source du tremblement de terre obtenu34 pour les près de 2 300 tremblements de terre survenus au cours de la période 2008–2019 (notes supplémentaires 3 et 4 et donnée supplémentaire 1).

tu.prop: We computed the volumes of both the fault cores and the damage zones of the seismogenic faults, starting from the cata- logue of the earthquake source parameters obtained34 for the ~2300 earthquakes that occurred during 2008–2019 (Supple- mentary Note 3 and 4 and Supplementary Data 1).

These values are orders of magnitude lower that the annual whole-crust volume of the IRPZ (the volume that contains all the hypocentres of the annual seismicity), which ranged from 7.4 × 104 km3 in 2018 to 1.2 × 105 km3 in 2012 (Supplementary Fig. 1).

tuv.seg: The volume of the fault core (Vfc) was calculated for each event, and then the annual sum was computed (Supplementary note 3), which yielded Vfc values ranging from 4.4 × 10–8 km3 (sum of Vfc in 2008) to 1.9 × 10–6 km3 (sum of Vfc in 2019).

Le volume du noyau de faille (Vfc) a été calculé pour chaque événement, puis la somme annuelle a été calculée (note supplémentaire 3), ce qui a donné des valeurs de Vfc allant de 4,4 × 10–8 km3 (somme de Vfc en 2008) à 1,9 × 10–6 km3 (somme de Vfc en 2019).

tu.prop: The volume of the fault core (Vfc) was calculated for each event, and then the annual sum was computed (Supplementary note 3), which yielded Vfc values ranging from 4.4 × 10–8 km3 (sum of Vfc in 2008) to 1.9 × 10–6 km3 (sum of Vfc in 2019).
The steady-state degassing in the core zone varies over two orders of magnitude, being between 3.9 × 10–10 and 1.5 × 10–8 mol y−1 (Fig. 3).

tuv.seg: These values are orders of magnitude lower that the annual whole-crust volume of the IRPZ (the volume that contains all the hypocentres of the annual seismicity), which ranged from 7.4 × 104 km3 in 2018 to 1.2 × 105 km3 in 2012 (Supplementary Fig. 1).

Ces valeurs sont inférieures de plusieurs ordres de grandeur au volume annuel de croûte entière de l'IRPZ (le volume qui contient tous les hypocentres de la sismicité annuelle), qui allait de 7,4 × 104 km3 en 2018 à 1,2 × 105 km3 en 2012 (Fig. Supplémentaire 1).

tu.prop: These values are orders of magnitude lower that the annual whole-crust volume of the IRPZ (the volume that contains all the hypocentres of the annual seismicity), which ranged from 7.4 × 104 km3 in 2018 to 1.2 × 105 km3 in 2012 (Supplementary Fig. 1).
However, this is 11–13 orders of magnitudes lower than the steady-state values in the IRPZ (1.2 × 103 to 4.8 × 103 mol y−1; Fig. 3).

tuv.seg: The steady-state degassing in the core zone varies over two orders of magnitude, being between 3.9 × 10–10 and 1.5 × 10–8 mol y−1 (Fig. 3).
Le dégazage à l'état stable dans la zone centrale varie sur deux ordres de grandeur, étant compris entre 3,9 × 10–10 et 1,5 × 10–8 mol y−1 (Fig. 3).

tu.prop: The steady-state degassing in the core zone varies over two orders of magnitude, being between 3.9 × 10–10 and 1.5 × 10–8 mol y−1 (Fig. 3).

Considering that the amount of 4He released from a deformed volume of rock is up to 104 times higher than the steady-state value for the same volume of rock23,32, even if the fault core is intensely deformed, the contribution of the fault-core crustal 4He to its regional degassing is still negligible, at up to 1.5 × 10–4 mol y−1 (Fig. 3).

tuv.seg: However, this is 11–13 orders of magnitudes lower than the steady-state values in the IRPZ (1.2 × 103 to 4.8 × 103 mol y−1; Fig. 3).
Cependant, il est inférieur de 11 à 13 ordres de grandeur aux valeurs en régime permanent dans l'IRPZ (1,2 × 103 à 4,8 × 103 mol y−1 ; Fig. 3).

tu.prop: However, this is 11–13 orders of magnitudes lower than the steady-state values in the IRPZ (1.2 × 103 to 4.8 × 103 mol y−1; Fig. 3).

In contrast, the estimated volume of the IRPZ damage zone ranges from 13.1 to 1.8 × 102 km3 (Supplementary Notes 4, Sup- plementary Table 1), which is up to seven orders of magnitude higher than that of the fault core (Supplementary Fig. 1).

tuv.seg: Considering that the amount of 4He released from a deformed volume of rock is up to 104 times higher than the steady-state value for the same volume of rock23,32, even if the fault core is intensely deformed, the contribution of the fault-core crustal 4He to its regional degassing is still negligible, at up to 1.5 × 10–4 mol y−1 (Fig. 3).

Étant donné que la quantité de 4He libérée par un volume déformé de roche est jusqu'à 104 fois supérieure à la valeur à l'état stable pour le même volume de roche23,32, même si le noyau de la faille est intensément déformé, la contribution du 4He crustal du noyau de la faille à son dégazage régional est encore négligeable, jusqu'à 1,5 × 10–4 mol y−1 (Fig. 3).

tu.prop: Considering that the amount of 4He released from a deformed volume of rock is up to 104 times higher than the steady-state value for the same volume of rock23,32, even if the fault core is intensely deformed, the contribution of the fault-core crustal 4He to its regional degassing is still negligible, at up to 1.5 × 10–4 mol y−1 (Fig. 3).

The steady-state output of crustal 4He from the IRPZ damage zone varies from 0.1 to 0.9 mol y−1(Supplementary Table 3), which is even lower than the regional steady-state whole-crust IRPZ degassing rate (1.2 to 2.0 × 103 mol y−1) (Fig. 3).

tuv.seg: In contrast, the estimated volume of the IRPZ damage zone ranges from 13.1 to 1.8 × 102 km3 (Supplementary Notes 4, Sup- plementary Table 1), which is up to seven orders of magnitude higher than that of the fault core (Supplementary Fig. 1).

En revanche, le volume estimé de la zone de dommages de l'IRPZ va de 13,1 à 1,8 × 102 km3 (Notes Supplémentaires 4, Tableau Supplémentaire 1), ce qui est jusqu'à sept ordres de grandeur supérieur à celui du noyau de faille (Fig. Supplémentaire 1).

tu.prop: In contrast, the estimated volume of the IRPZ damage zone ranges from 13.1 to 1.8 × 102 km3 (Supplementary Notes 4, Sup- plementary Table 1), which is up to seven orders of magnitude higher than that of the fault core (Supplementary Fig. 1).

It is worth noting that annually there is a tenfold variability in the absence of high- magnitude earthquakes (M < 4.4), supporting the impulsive nature of crustal 4He degassing associated with the nucleation of earth- quakes even in absence of high-magnitude earthquakes in the IRPZ.

tuv.seg: The steady-state output of crustal 4He from the IRPZ damage zone varies from 0.1 to 0.9 mol y−1(Supplementary Table 3), which is even lower than the regional steady-state whole-crust IRPZ degassing rate (1.2 to 2.0 × 103 mol y−1) (Fig. 3).

La production à l'état stable de 4He crustal dans la zone endommagée par l'IRPZ varie de 0,1 à 0,9 mol y−1 (tableau supplémentaire 3), ce qui est même inférieur au taux de dégazage régional à l'état stable de l'IRPZ à croûte entière (1,2 à 2,0 × 103 mol y−1) (Fig. 3).

tu.prop: The steady-state output of crustal 4He from the IRPZ damage zone varies from 0.1 to 0.9 mol y−1(Supplementary Table 3), which is even lower than the regional steady-state whole-crust IRPZ degassing rate (1.2 to 2.0 × 103 mol y−1) (Fig. 3).

Furthermore, considering the maximum release of 4He from the volume of the damage zone due to rock deformation (up to 104 times the steady-state release)23,32, the annual degassing rate between 2008 and 2019 for the damage zone is up to 0.9 × 104 mol y−1.

tuv.seg: It is worth noting that annually there is a tenfold variability in the absence of high- magnitude earthquakes (M < 4.4), supporting the impulsive nature of crustal 4He degassing associated with the nucleation of earth- quakes even in absence of high-magnitude earthquakes in the IRPZ.

Il convient de noter qu'il existe chaque année une variabilité décuplée en l'absence de séismes de grande magnitude (M < 4,4), ce qui confirme la nature impulsive du dégazage du 4He crustal associé à la nucléation des séismes, même en l'absence de séismes de grande magnitude dans l'IRPZ.

tu.prop: It is worth noting that annually there is a tenfold variability in the absence of high- magnitude earthquakes (M < 4.4), supporting the impulsive nature of crustal 4He degassing associated with the nucleation of earth- quakes even in absence of high-magnitude earthquakes in the IRPZ.

This value is equal to or higher than the regional steady-state degassing rate from the whole non-deformed 4 COMMUNICATIONS EARTH & ENVIRONMENT | (2022) 3:224 | https://doi.org/10.1038/s43247-022-00549-9 | www.nature.com/commsenv

tuv.seg: Furthermore, considering the maximum release of 4He from the volume of the damage zone due to rock deformation (up to 104 times the steady-state release)23,32, the annual degassing rate between 2008 and 2019 for the damage zone is up to 0.9 × 104 mol y−1.

En outre, compte tenu de la libération maximale de 4He selon le volume de la zone endommagée due à la déformation de la roche (jusqu'à 104 fois la libération à l'état stable)23,32, le taux de dégazage annuel entre 2008 et 2019 pour la zone endommagée est jusqu'à 0,9 × 104 mol y−1.

tu.prop: Furthermore, considering the maximum release of 4He from the volume of the damage zone due to rock deformation (up to 104 times the steady-state release)23,32, the annual degassing rate between 2008 and 2019 for the damage zone is up to 0.9 × 104 mol y−1.

These values of the crustal 4He output are computed by using (1) the U and Th concentrations in the regional lithology, (2) the areas of the annual epicentres and 3) the crustal thickness below IRPZ (all the data and the equations used in the computations are in Supplementary Note 1–3).

For comparison, we also computed the annual steady state output (2008–2019) of the crustal 4He in IRPZ by using the typical range of the U and Th concentrations in the continental crust (Table 1).

tuv.seg: These values of the crustal 4He output are computed by using (1) the U and Th concentrations in the regional lithology, (2) the areas of the annual epicentres and 3) the crustal thickness below IRPZ (all the data and the equations used in the computations are in Supplementary Note 1–3).

Ces valeurs de la production de 4He dans la croûte sont calculées en utilisant (1) les concentrations d'U et de Th dans la lithologie régionale, (2) les zones des épicentres annuels et (3) l'épaisseur de la croûte sous l'IRPZ (toutes les données et les équations utilisées dans les calculs figurent dans les notes supplémentaires 1 à 3).

tu.prop: These values of the crustal 4He output are computed by using (1) the U and Th concentrations in the regional lithology, (2) the areas of the annual epicentres and 3) the crustal thickness below IRPZ (all the data and the equations used in the computations are in Supplementary Note 1–3).
The blue columns represent the steady state annual output of crustal 4He from the volumes of rocks that characterize the IRPZ faults cores.

tuv.seg: For comparison, we also computed the annual steady state output (2008–2019) of the crustal 4He in IRPZ by using the typical range of the U and Th concentrations in the continental crust (Table 1).

À titre de comparaison, nous avons également estimé la production annuelle de 4He crustal en régime permanent (2008-2019) dans l'IRPZ en utilisant la gamme typique des concentrations d'U et de Th dans la croûte continentale (tableau 1).

tu.prop: For comparison, we also computed the annual steady state output (2008–2019) of the crustal 4He in IRPZ by using the typical range of the U and Th concentrations in the continental crust (Table 1).
Here we used the U and Th concentrations of the lithology that constitute the faults cores (Table 1).

The mantle 4He output the New Zealand and Iceland volcanic-related geothermal ﬁelds and the Italian volcanic systems are computed by the CO2 outputs54, the mantle C/3He ratios55, and the 3He/4He ratio of these area43,56–60. crust in the IRPZ (Fig. 3).
