tu.prop: The 4He output at the Yellowstone Park are from Lowenstern et al., (2014)24.
It should be noted that seismicity in the IRPZ is mainly concentrated within the limestones, which are U- and Th-poor lithology41 (Table 1).

tuv.seg: The mantle 4He output the New Zealand and Iceland volcanic-related geothermal ﬁelds and the Italian volcanic systems are computed by the CO2 outputs54, the mantle C/3He ratios55, and the 3He/4He ratio of these area43,56–60. crust in the IRPZ (Fig. 3).

Les données de 4He mantélique des champs géothermiques liés aux volcans de Nouvelle-Zélande et d'Islande et des systèmes volcaniques italiens est calculée à partir des données en CO254, les rapports C/3He du manteau55 et le rapport 3He/4He de ces zones43,56-60. de la croûte non-déformée dans l'IRPZ (Fig. 3).

tu.prop: The mantle 4He output the New Zealand and Iceland volcanic-related geothermal ﬁelds and the Italian volcanic systems are computed by the CO2 outputs54, the mantle C/3He ratios55, and the 3He/4He ratio of these area43,56–60. crust in the IRPZ (Fig. 3).

Therefore, notwithstanding (1) a low crustal 4He production due to the low amount of U and Th in the seismogenic volume of rocks and (2) an absence of high- magnitude earthquakes, the degassing rate of crustal 4He from the seismogenic IRPZ crust, which is so high because of the intense deformation of the rock that increase the release of 4He from the rock, (Fig. 3) can be at least equal to that due to the whole and not-deformed IRPZ continental crust (Fig. 3). the Considering a damage zone involving U- and Th-rich rocks, such as granite and gabbro that are typical in continental regions (4 and 52 ppm U and 1 and 3.5 ppm Th)49, and assuming a volumetric expansion of the damage zone as for the IRPZ, the output of crustal 4He would be up to ~106 and ~105 mol y-1, respectively (Supplementary Figs.

tuv.seg: It should be noted that seismicity in the IRPZ is mainly concentrated within the limestones, which are U- and Th-poor lithology41 (Table 1).
Il convient de noter que la sismicité dans l'IRPZ est principalement concentrée dans les calcaires, qui sont des lithologies pauvres en U et Th41 (Tableau 1).

tu.prop: It should be noted that seismicity in the IRPZ is mainly concentrated within the limestones, which are U- and Th-poor lithology41 (Table 1).

tuv.seg: Therefore, notwithstanding (1) a low crustal 4He production due to the low amount of U and Th in the seismogenic volume of rocks and (2) an absence of high- magnitude earthquakes, the degassing rate of crustal 4He from the seismogenic IRPZ crust, which is so high because of the intense deformation of the rock that increase the release of 4He from the rock, (Fig. 3) can be at least equal to that due to the whole and not-deformed IRPZ continental crust (Fig. 3). the Considering a damage zone involving U- and Th-rich rocks, such as granite and gabbro that are typical in continental regions (4 and 52 ppm U and 1 and 3.5 ppm Th)49, and assuming a volumetric expansion of the damage zone as for the IRPZ, the output of crustal 4He would be up to ~106 and ~105 mol y-1, respectively (Supplementary Figs.

Par conséquent, malgré (1) une faible production de 4He crustal en raison de la faible quantité d'U et de Th dans le volume sismogène des roches et (2) une absence de séismes de grande magnitude, le taux de dégazage de 4He crustal à partir de la croûte sismogène IRPZ, qui est si élevé en raison de la déformation intense de la roche qui augmente la libération de 4He à partir de la roche, (Fig. 3) peut être au moins égal à celui formé dans la croûte continentale IRPZ entière et non déformée (Fig. 3). Considérant une zone de dommages impliquant des roches riches en U et en Th, telles que le granite et le gabbro qui sont typiques dans les régions continentales (4 et 52 ppm U et 1 et 3,5 ppm Th)49, et en supposant une expansion volumétrique de la zone de dommages comme pour l'IRPZ, la quantité de 4He crustal serait jusqu'à environ 106 et environ 105 mol y-1, respectivement (Figures supplémentaires.

tu.prop: Therefore, notwithstanding (1) a low crustal 4He production due to the low amount of U and Th in the seismogenic volume of rocks and (2) an absence of high- magnitude earthquakes, the degassing rate of crustal 4He from the seismogenic IRPZ crust, which is so high because of the intense deformation of the rock that increase the release of 4He from the rock, (Fig. 3) can be at least equal to that due to the whole and not-deformed IRPZ continental crust (Fig. 3). the Considering a damage zone involving U- and Th-rich rocks, such as granite and gabbro that are typical in continental regions (4 and 52 ppm U and 1 and 3.5 ppm Th)49, and assuming a volumetric expansion of the damage zone as for the IRPZ, the output of crustal 4He would be up to ~106 and ~105 mol y-1, respectively (Supplementary Figs.
Therefore, a volume of granitic crust in a strongly deformed state can degas up to ~107 moles of crustal 4He over 10 years.

tuv.seg: 2 and 3).
2 et 3)

tu.prop: This amount of out- gassing would correspond to ~56% of the crustal 4He degassed during 1995 earthquake (1.8 × 107 mol)29 and to ~1.8% of the worldwide steady-state global degassing (5.4 × 108 mol y−1)29. in Kobe disastrous It is worth noting that the values of crustal 4He released from a damage zone in granitic rock overlap the high value of crustal 4He output from the large high-temperature geothermal systems worldwide (e.g. Iceland, New Zealand) and the active volcanoes of the systems of the southern of Italy (Fig. 3).

tuv.seg: Therefore, a volume of granitic crust in a strongly deformed state can degas up to ~107 moles of crustal 4He over 10 years.
Par conséquent, un volume de croûte granitique à l'état fortement déformé peut dégazer jusqu'à près de 107 moles de 4He crustal sur 10 ans.

tu.prop: Therefore, a volume of granitic crust in a strongly deformed state can degas up to ~107 moles of crustal 4He over 10 years.

However, these values are still lower than the prodigious emission of crustal 4He at Yellowstone National Park, where crustal metamorphism induced by a deep hotspot liberates the 4He that has accumulated over more than 2.5 billion years24.

tuv.seg: This amount of out- gassing would correspond to ~56% of the crustal 4He degassed during 1995 earthquake (1.8 × 107 mol)29 and to ~1.8% of the worldwide steady-state global degassing (5.4 × 108 mol y−1)29. in Kobe disastrous It is worth noting that the values of crustal 4He released from a damage zone in granitic rock overlap the high value of crustal 4He output from the large high-temperature geothermal systems worldwide (e.g. Iceland, New Zealand) and the active volcanoes of the systems of the southern of Italy (Fig. 3).

Cette quantité de dégazage correspondrait à environ 56 % du 4He crustal dégazé lors du séisme désastreux de Kobe de 1995 (1,8 × 107 mol)29 et à environ 1,8 % du dégazage mondial à l'état stable (5,4 × 108 mol y−1)29. Il convient de noter que les valeurs du 4He crustal libéré par une zone endommagée dans la roche granitique chevauchent la valeur élevée du 4He crustal produit par les grands systèmes géothermiques à haute température dans le monde (par exemple, l'Islande, la Nouvelle-Zélande) et les volcans actifs des systèmes du sud de l'Italie (Fig. 3).

tu.prop: This amount of out- gassing would correspond to ~56% of the crustal 4He degassed during 1995 earthquake (1.8 × 107 mol)29 and to ~1.8% of the worldwide steady-state global degassing (5.4 × 108 mol y−1)29. in Kobe disastrous It is worth noting that the values of crustal 4He released from a damage zone in granitic rock overlap the high value of crustal 4He output from the large high-temperature geothermal systems worldwide (e.g. Iceland, New Zealand) and the active volcanoes of the systems of the southern of Italy (Fig. 3).

This evidence strongly reinforces the present ﬁnding that earthquake nucleation plays an active role in the impulsive nature of crustal 4He degassing even in the absence of high-magnitude earthquakes. in In this scenario, crustal 4He from the seismogenic rock volumes (Fig. 3) strongly contributes to diluting the mantle- derived He that has been recognized in worldwide regions asso- ciated with high-magnitude earthquakes (e.g. San Andreas fault, USA; southwest Japan; Anatolian fault, Turkey; Belice valley, Italy)20,30,50–52.

tuv.seg: However, these values are still lower than the prodigious emission of crustal 4He at Yellowstone National Park, where crustal metamorphism induced by a deep hotspot liberates the 4He that has accumulated over more than 2.5 billion years24.

Cependant, ces valeurs sont encore inférieures à l'émission prodigieuse de 4He crustal au parc national de Yellowstone, où le métamorphisme crustal induit par un point chaud profond libère le 4He qui s'est accumulé depuis plus de 2,5 milliards d'années24.

tu.prop: However, these values are still lower than the prodigious emission of crustal 4He at Yellowstone National Park, where crustal metamorphism induced by a deep hotspot liberates the 4He that has accumulated over more than 2.5 billion years24.
According to our results, the contribution from earthquake-related crustal 4He varies over time and is dependent upon the extension of fault zones, their lithology and the earth- quake magnitude.

tuv.seg: This evidence strongly reinforces the present ﬁnding that earthquake nucleation plays an active role in the impulsive nature of crustal 4He degassing even in the absence of high-magnitude earthquakes. in In this scenario, crustal 4He from the seismogenic rock volumes (Fig. 3) strongly contributes to diluting the mantle- derived He that has been recognized in worldwide regions asso- ciated with high-magnitude earthquakes (e.g. San Andreas fault, USA; southwest Japan; Anatolian fault, Turkey; Belice valley, Italy)20,30,50–52.

Ces preuves renforcent fortement la conclusion actuelle selon laquelle la nucléation sismique joue un rôle actif dans la nature impulsive du dégazage de 4He crustal, même en l'absence de séismes de forte magnitude.Dans ce scénario, le 4He crustal provenant des volumes de roches sismogènes (Fig. 3) contribue fortement à diluer le He dérivé du manteau qui a été reconnu dans les régions du monde associées à des séismes de forte magnitude (par exemple, la faille de San Andreas aux États-Unis, le sud-ouest du Japon, la faille anatolienne en Turquie, la vallée de Belice en Italie).

tu.prop: This evidence strongly reinforces the present ﬁnding that earthquake nucleation plays an active role in the impulsive nature of crustal 4He degassing even in the absence of high-magnitude earthquakes. in In this scenario, crustal 4He from the seismogenic rock volumes (Fig. 3) strongly contributes to diluting the mantle- derived He that has been recognized in worldwide regions asso- ciated with high-magnitude earthquakes (e.g. San Andreas fault, USA; southwest Japan; Anatolian fault, Turkey; Belice valley, Italy)20,30,50–52.

These ﬁndings highlight that seismicity plays an active role in the high variability of the He isotopic signature in seismic zones, and they contribute quantitatively to explaining this variability over short distances along different sectors of faults4,20,42,52,53.

tuv.seg: According to our results, the contribution from earthquake-related crustal 4He varies over time and is dependent upon the extension of fault zones, their lithology and the earth- quake magnitude.
Selon nos résultats, la contribution de l'4He crustal liée aux séismes varie dans le temps et dépend de l'extension des zones de faille, de leur lithologie et de la magnitude du séisme.

tu.prop: According to our results, the contribution from earthquake-related crustal 4He varies over time and is dependent upon the extension of fault zones, their lithology and the earth- quake magnitude.

Hence, our results clearly indicate that an increase of the crustal 4He component over time can shift the He isotopic signature in the natural ﬂuids released in seismic regions towards a radiogenic endmember (0.01–0.05 Ra), which is indi- cative of changes in the crustal stress, and hence provides hints about the preparatory phases of large earthquakes. (e.g. Conclusions and future outlooks.

tuv.seg: These ﬁndings highlight that seismicity plays an active role in the high variability of the He isotopic signature in seismic zones, and they contribute quantitatively to explaining this variability over short distances along different sectors of faults4,20,42,52,53.

Ces résultats mettent en lumière le rôle actif de la sismicité dans la grande variabilité de la signature isotopique de l'hélium dans les zones sismiques, et ils fournissent des résultats quantitatifs qui permettent d'expliquer cette variabilité sur de courtes distances le long de différents secteurs de failles4,20,42,52,53.

tu.prop: These ﬁndings highlight that seismicity plays an active role in the high variability of the He isotopic signature in seismic zones, and they contribute quantitatively to explaining this variability over short distances along different sectors of faults4,20,42,52,53.

Records of sharp increases in crustal 4He in natural ﬂuids due to rock microscale fracturation earthquake-related are rare, and the previous records come exclusively from high-magnitude earthquakes that of 1.8 × 107 mol in the Kobe earthquake in 1995)29.

tuv.seg: Hence, our results clearly indicate that an increase of the crustal 4He component over time can shift the He isotopic signature in the natural ﬂuids released in seismic regions towards a radiogenic endmember (0.01–0.05 Ra), which is indi- cative of changes in the crustal stress, and hence provides hints about the preparatory phases of large earthquakes. (e.g. Conclusions and future outlooks.

Ainsi, nos résultats indiquent clairement qu'une augmentation de l'4He crustal au fil du temps peut changer la signature isotopique de l'hélium dans les fluides naturels libérés dans les régions sismiques jusqu'à atteindre un extrême radiogénique (0,01–0,05 Ra), ce qui indique des changements dans les contraintes crustales, et donc fournit des indices sur les phases préparatoires des séismes de grande ampleur. (par exemple les conclusions et futures recherches)

tu.prop: Hence, our results clearly indicate that an increase of the crustal 4He component over time can shift the He isotopic signature in the natural ﬂuids released in seismic regions towards a radiogenic endmember (0.01–0.05 Ra), which is indi- cative of changes in the crustal stress, and hence provides hints about the preparatory phases of large earthquakes. (e.g. Conclusions and future outlooks.
Our results indicate that the background seismicity during interseismic per- iods also controls the impulsive degassing of crustal 4He.

tuv.seg: Records of sharp increases in crustal 4He in natural ﬂuids due to rock microscale fracturation earthquake-related are rare, and the previous records come exclusively from high-magnitude earthquakes that of 1.8 × 107 mol in the Kobe earthquake in 1995)29.

Les enregistrements d'augmentations abruptes du 4He crustal dans les fluides naturels en raison de la fracturation à micro-échelle des roches liée aux séismes sont rares, et les enregistrements précédents proviennent exclusivement de séismes de grande magnitude (par exemple, celui de 1,8 × 107 mol lors du séisme de Kobe en 1995) [29].

tu.prop: Records of sharp increases in crustal 4He in natural ﬂuids due to rock microscale fracturation earthquake-related are rare, and the previous records come exclusively from high-magnitude earthquakes that of 1.8 × 107 mol in the Kobe earthquake in 1995)29.

Therefore, coupling geochemical and geophysical long series of data, the crustal 4He outputs can be used as an indicator of the expansion of the seismogenic process, thereby making it possible to assess the volumes of deformed rocks along the faults.

tuv.seg: Our results indicate that the background seismicity during interseismic per- iods also controls the impulsive degassing of crustal 4He.
Nos résultats indiquent que la sismicité de fond pendant les périodes inter-sismiques contrôle également le dégazage impulsionnel du 4He crustal.

tu.prop: Our results indicate that the background seismicity during interseismic per- iods also controls the impulsive degassing of crustal 4He.

Our study indicates that a high frequency He monitoring is funda- mental to examine and calibrate regional models capable of and describing between He relationship degassing the COMMUNICATIONS EARTH & ENVIRONMENT | (2022) 3:224 | https://doi.org/10.1038/s43247-022-00549-9 | www.nature.com/commsenv 5

tuv.seg: Therefore, coupling geochemical and geophysical long series of data, the crustal 4He outputs can be used as an indicator of the expansion of the seismogenic process, thereby making it possible to assess the volumes of deformed rocks along the faults.

Par conséquent, en couplant des longues séries de données géochimiques et géophysiques, les productions d'4He crustal peuvent être utilisées comme indicateur de l'expansion du processus sismogénique, ce qui permet d'évaluer les volumes de roches déformées le long des failles.

tu.prop: Therefore, coupling geochemical and geophysical long series of data, the crustal 4He outputs can be used as an indicator of the expansion of the seismogenic process, thereby making it possible to assess the volumes of deformed rocks along the faults.

Therefore, as already highlighted in volcanic surveillance15–17, our study emphasizes the need to use new, ﬁeld deployable analytical solutions that can allow He data to be acquired (amounts and isotopic signature) with a frequency from weekly to daily.
These new data could help to reconstruct the temporal evolution of natural processes such as volcanic eruption and earthquakes.

tuv.seg: Therefore, as already highlighted in volcanic surveillance15–17, our study emphasizes the need to use new, ﬁeld deployable analytical solutions that can allow He data to be acquired (amounts and isotopic signature) with a frequency from weekly to daily.

Par conséquent, comme déjà souligné dans la surveillance volcanique [15], [16],[17], notre étude met en avant la nécessité d'utiliser de nouvelles solutions analytiques déployables sur le terrain qui permettent d'acquérir des données sur l'hélium (quantités et signature isotopique) à une fréquence allant d'hebdomadaire à quotidienne.

tu.prop: Therefore, as already highlighted in volcanic surveillance15–17, our study emphasizes the need to use new, ﬁeld deployable analytical solutions that can allow He data to be acquired (amounts and isotopic signature) with a frequency from weekly to daily.

This study highlights that a continuous, multidisciplinary, integrated monitoring approach that includes geochemical and geophysical observations can be the key to understanding the mechanisms underlying earthquake generation, and could facil- itate to ﬁgure out premonitory rock deformation processes preceding the occurrence of a disastrous earthquake.

tuv.seg: These new data could help to reconstruct the temporal evolution of natural processes such as volcanic eruption and earthquakes.
Ces nouvelles données pourraient aider à reconstituer l'évolution temporelle des processus naturels tels que les éruptions volcaniques et les séismes.

tu.prop: These new data could help to reconstruct the temporal evolution of natural processes such as volcanic eruption and earthquakes.
Therefore, the novel results obtained in this study provide a new paradigm for studying the genesis of earthquakes and integrating multi- disciplinary data for improving earthquake forecasts.

tuv.seg: This study highlights that a continuous, multidisciplinary, integrated monitoring approach that includes geochemical and geophysical observations can be the key to understanding the mechanisms underlying earthquake generation, and could facil- itate to ﬁgure out premonitory rock deformation processes preceding the occurrence of a disastrous earthquake.

Cette étude souligne qu'une approche de suivi continue, multidisciplinaire et intégrée comprenant des observations géochimiques et géophysiques peut être la clé pour comprendre les mécanismes sous-jacents à la création de séismes, et pourrait faciliter la compréhension des processus de déformation rocheuse précurseurs de la survenue d'un séisme catastrophique.

tu.prop: This study highlights that a continuous, multidisciplinary, integrated monitoring approach that includes geochemical and geophysical observations can be the key to understanding the mechanisms underlying earthquake generation, and could facil- itate to ﬁgure out premonitory rock deformation processes preceding the occurrence of a disastrous earthquake.
Methods Whole crust He degassing in steady-state conditions.

tuv.seg: Therefore, the novel results obtained in this study provide a new paradigm for studying the genesis of earthquakes and integrating multi- disciplinary data for improving earthquake forecasts.

Par conséquent, les résultats novateurs obtenus lors de cette étude fournissent un nouveau paradigme pour l'étude de la genèse des séismes et l'intégration de données multidisciplinaires afin d'améliorer les prévisions sismiques.

tu.prop: Therefore, the novel results obtained in this study provide a new paradigm for studying the genesis of earthquakes and integrating multi- disciplinary data for improving earthquake forecasts.

On a 1-Ma timescale, the ﬂux of 4He from the Earth’s crust to the atmosphere is comparable to the net in situ production by alpha decay of U- and Th-series elements at the 30–40 km depth of the crust54, suggesting that the 4He ﬂux exists in the crust and eventually reaches the atmosphere with a steady ﬂux Liu et al.

tuv.seg: Methods Whole crust He degassing in steady-state conditions.
Méthodes : le dégazage du He de toute la croûte dans des conditions stables.

tu.prop: Methods Whole crust He degassing in steady-state conditions.

tuv.seg: On a 1-Ma timescale, the ﬂux of 4He from the Earth’s crust to the atmosphere is comparable to the net in situ production by alpha decay of U- and Th-series elements at the 30–40 km depth of the crust54, suggesting that the 4He ﬂux exists in the crust and eventually reaches the atmosphere with a steady ﬂux Liu et al.

Sur une échelle de temps de 1 Ma, le flux de 4He de la croûte terrestre vers l'atmosphère est comparable à la production nette in situ par désintégration alpha des éléments des séries U et Th à la profondeur de 30 à 40 km de la croûte [54], ce qui suggère que le flux de 4He existe dans la croûte et finit par atteindre l'atmosphère avec un flux stable (Liu et al.)

tu.prop: On a 1-Ma timescale, the ﬂux of 4He from the Earth’s crust to the atmosphere is comparable to the net in situ production by alpha decay of U- and Th-series elements at the 30–40 km depth of the crust54, suggesting that the 4He ﬂux exists in the crust and eventually reaches the atmosphere with a steady ﬂux Liu et al.

The rate of 4He production (and release in S.S. conditions) due to α-decay of U and Th throughout the crust beneath the study area (QC He in moles per year) is equivalent to24: ¼ Qc;y Mc;y NA (cid:2) α ¼ δ (cid:2) ðSc;y (cid:2) Hc c Þ (cid:2) α ð1Þ where Mc,y is the annual mass of crust beneath the IRPZ in grams, calculated as the product of the density and volume of the crust beneath the study area, which is calculated as the product of the annual surface area (Sc,y) and the crustal thickness, and according to the local geological sections41 this latter is given by the sum of carbonate, middle crust and lower crust thicknesses (HC = Hc + HMC + HLC).
NA is the Avogadro constant and α is the crustal production of 4He in molgrams per year.

tuv.seg: The rate of 4He production (and release in S.S. conditions) due to α-decay of U and Th throughout the crust beneath the study area (QC He in moles per year) is equivalent to24: ¼ Qc;y Mc;y NA (cid:2) α ¼ δ (cid:2) ðSc;y (cid:2) Hc c Þ (cid:2) α ð1Þ where Mc,y is the annual mass of crust beneath the IRPZ in grams, calculated as the product of the density and volume of the crust beneath the study area, which is calculated as the product of the annual surface area (Sc,y) and the crustal thickness, and according to the local geological sections41 this latter is given by the sum of carbonate, middle crust and lower crust thicknesses (HC = Hc + HMC + HLC).

Le taux de production de 4He (et de libération dans des conditions à l'état stable) en raison de la désintégration α de U et de Th dans toute la croûte sous la zone d'étude (QC He en moles par an) est équivalent à 24 : ¼ Qc ;y Mc ;y NA (cid :2) α ¼ δ (cid :2) ðSc ;y (cid :2) Hc c Þ (cid :2) α ð1Þ où Mc,y est la masse annuelle de croûte sous l'IRPZ en grammes, calculée comme le produit de la densité et du volume de la croûte sous la zone d'étude, qui est calculé comme le produit de la surface annuelle (Sc,y) et de l'épaisseur de la croûte, et selon les sections géologiques locales41, cette dernière est donnée par la somme des épaisseurs de carbonate, de croûte moyenne et de croûte inférieure (HC = Hc + HMC + HLC).

Regarding the ﬁrst and second set, we base our analysis on the U and Th amounts for a Regional Reﬁned Reference Model and the Global Reﬁned Reference Model proposed by Coltorti et al.

tu.prop: In particular, we use three sets of U and Th concentrations for estimating the Steady-State level for area.

tuv.seg: Regarding the ﬁrst and second set, we base our analysis on the U and Th amounts for a Regional Reﬁned Reference Model and the Global Reﬁned Reference Model proposed by Coltorti et al.

En ce qui concerne les premier et deuxième ensembles, nous basons notre analyse sur les montants U et Th pour un Modèle de Référence Régional Affiné et le Modèle de Référence Mondial Affiné proposé par Coltorti et al.

tu.prop: Regarding the ﬁrst and second set, we base our analysis on the U and Th amounts for a Regional Reﬁned Reference Model and the Global Reﬁned Reference Model proposed by Coltorti et al.
For the third set (Global O-O steady state), we refer to the values proposed by O’Nions and Oxburgh 1983)19.
Thereafter, we will refer to the result of these three conﬁgurations as the Regional, global and O-O steady-state, respectively.

tuv.seg: For the third set (Global O-O steady state), we refer to the values proposed by O’Nions and Oxburgh 1983)19.
Pour le troisième ensemble (état d'équilibre O-O global), nous nous référons aux valeurs proposées par O’Nions et Oxburgh (1983) [19].

tu.prop: For the third set (Global O-O steady state), we refer to the values proposed by O’Nions and Oxburgh 1983)19.
In addition, in the regional case, we considered granites and gabbro as alternatives in place of carbonates (see Table 1 for U and Th abundance and Supplementary Figs.

tuv.seg: Thereafter, we will refer to the result of these three conﬁgurations as the Regional, global and O-O steady-state, respectively.
Par la suite, nous nous référerons au résultat de ces trois conﬁgurations comme étant l'état stable régional, respectivement global et O-O.

tu.prop: Thereafter, we will refer to the result of these three conﬁgurations as the Regional, global and O-O steady-state, respectively.

De plus, dans le cas régional, nous avons considéré les granites et le gabbro comme des alternatives à la place des carbonates (voir le tableau 1 pour l'abondance en U et Th et les chiffres complémentaires.

tuv.seg: 4He degassing in no-steady-state conditions.
Dégazage du 4He dans des conditions non stationnaires.
To estimate the crustal 4He epi- sodic release due to seismic activity, we applied Eq. (2) to the volume related to the fault core and the damage zone (obtained from Supplementary Eq.

Pour estimer la libération épisodique crustale d'4He due à l'activité sismique, nous avons appliqué l'équation (2) au volume lié au cœur de la faille et à la zone endommagée (obtenue à partir de l'équation complémentaire).

tu.prop: To estimate the crustal 4He epi- sodic release due to seismic activity, we applied Eq. (2) to the volume related to the fault core and the damage zone (obtained from Supplementary Eq.
2 respectively), by which we obtain the rate of 4He release from fault core (Qfc,y) and damage zone (Qdz,y) (Supplementary Table 3).

1 et Eq.

tu.prop: Volatile release from the rock increases as a consequence of dilatancy, and in regions affected by active tectonics the ﬂux of 4He through the crust should be higher than in volume of rocks without deformation, where only a steady-state transport system is expected to act27.

tuv.seg: 2 respectively), by which we obtain the rate of 4He release from fault core (Qfc,y) and damage zone (Qdz,y) (Supplementary Table 3).
2 respectivement), par lequel nous obtenons le taux de libération d'4He à partir du noyau de la faille (Qfc,y) et de la zone endommagée (Qdz,y) (tableau supplémentaire 3).

tu.prop: 2 respectively), by which we obtain the rate of 4He release from fault core (Qfc,y) and damage zone (Qdz,y) (Supplementary Table 3).

So, the release of 4He from rock, which is affected by dilatancy, is from 10 to 104 times higher than that in un-deformed rock32 (i.e., of Qfc,y and Qdz,y values, see Table 2), see main text for insights.

tuv.seg: Volatile release from the rock increases as a consequence of dilatancy, and in regions affected by active tectonics the ﬂux of 4He through the crust should be higher than in volume of rocks without deformation, where only a steady-state transport system is expected to act27.

La libération volatile de la roche augmente en raison de la dilatance, et dans les régions touchées par la tectonique active, le flux de 4He à travers la croûte devrait être plus élevé que le volume des roches sans déformation, où seul un système de transport à l'état stable devrait agir [27].

tu.prop: Volatile release from the rock increases as a consequence of dilatancy, and in regions affected by active tectonics the ﬂux of 4He through the crust should be higher than in volume of rocks without deformation, where only a steady-state transport system is expected to act27.
Data availability The authors declare that the data supporting the ﬁndings of this study are available within the article and its Supplementary Information (see also the Methods section).

tuv.seg: So, the release of 4He from rock, which is affected by dilatancy, is from 10 to 104 times higher than that in un-deformed rock32 (i.e., of Qfc,y and Qdz,y values, see Table 2), see main text for insights.

Ainsi, la libération d'4He à partir de la roche, qui est affectée par la dilatance, est de 10 à 104 fois supérieure à celle dans la roche non déformée32 (c.-à-d., des valeurs Qfc,y et Qdz,y, voir le tableau 2), voir le texte principal pour plus d'informations.

The raw data (seismic waveforms) for the Irpinia Seismic Network, ISNet (i.e., FDSN code IX), are available from the Observatories and Research Facilities for European Seismology European Integrated Data Archive (URL, https://www.orfeus-eu.org/data/ eida/).
